target.title: fix(cron): recover from local scope upgrades
target.body:
## Summary

- Request `operator.write` for `cron.add`, `cron.update`, `cron.remove`, and `cron.run` instead of `operator.admin`, so local cron mutations fit the normal write-scoped operator baseline.
- Teach cron CLI error handling to recognize pairing and scope-upgrade transport failures, then print exact `openclaw devices approve ...` and `openclaw devices list` recovery commands.
- Keep cron CLI JSON errors machine-readable and update the operator-scope docs/changelog.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli/shared.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled npx --yes pnpm@11.2.2 check:changed`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/operator-scopes.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A local cron CLI command that reconnects with broader cron mutation scopes should ask for normal `operator.write` access and, when the Gateway requires a scope upgrade approval, should show the exact device approval command instead of a raw WebSocket close error.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, running the actual `openclaw cron add` CLI entrypoint through `npx --yes pnpm@11.2.2 openclaw`, connected to a live local WebSocket listener that closes with the Gateway scope-upgrade close reason and `requestId`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ node tmp/proof-cron-scope-upgrade.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local source CLI:

```console
$ npx --yes pnpm@11.2.2 openclaw cron add --url ws://127.0.0.1:19876 --token proof-token --name scope-upgrade-proof --every 1h --message prove cron scope upgrade guidance
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs cron add --url ws://127.0.0.1:19876 --token proof-token --name scope-upgrade-proof --every 1h --message 'prove cron scope upgrade guidance'

```

- **What was not tested:** A full persisted Gateway device-pairing store upgrade was not exercised in this fork PR; the terminal capture uses the same Gateway scope-upgrade close reason over a live local WebSocket to prove the CLI recovery text.

target.title: fix(codex): cap managed app-server trace logs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes/ref: #86214

Adds startup retention for OpenClaw-managed Codex app-server trace logs under `CODEX_HOME`:

- applies before shared and isolated Codex app-server startup
- rotates `logs_2.sqlite`, `logs_2.sqlite-wal`, and `logs_2.sqlite-shm` when their combined size exceeds the cap
- defaults to a 512 MiB cap and keeps the 2 newest retired snapshots
- exposes and documents env controls for disable/max-bytes/snapshot count
- logs a warning when rotation happens, but does not fail app-server startup if retention itself fails

This deliberately bounds OpenClaw-managed storage without needing to know the native Codex SQLite schema.

## Why

In one affected OpenClaw install, the managed Codex DB grew to ~43 GiB / 14.5M rows in under 8 days, while standalone Codex on the same machine had only an ~81 MiB `~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite`.

The live process chain was:

`openclaw gateway -> codex.js app-server -> native codex app-server --listen stdio://`

The growing file was:

`~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/logs_2.sqlite`

Additional evidence was posted on #86214:

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86214#issuecomment-4530473714

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw-managed Codex app-server log storage must not grow without a bounded retention path when `logs_2.sqlite` accumulates high-volume response/WebSocket/SSE trace rows.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw checkout on commit `d239d37f18`, using the PR branch's actual `createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient` startup path with a realistic managed per-agent `CODEX_HOME` containing `logs_2.sqlite` and `logs_2.sqlite-wal`. The app-server command was a real stdio subprocess launched by Node, not a mocked `CodexAppServerClient.start`. Supporting production context comes from the affected Mac mini OpenClaw install that produced the ~43 GiB managed `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/logs_2.sqlite`.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix:** Created a realistic managed `CODEX_HOME`, wrote oversized `logs_2.sqlite` / WAL files, set `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=4`, then launched a fake stdio app-server subprocess through the branch's `createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient`. The fake app-server wrote its startup observation before responding to `initialize`. The committed equivalent verification command is:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts --testNamePattern "rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts" --reporter verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the direct branch run showed the startup retention warning and the fake app-server's observed startup state. The original database was gone before the app-server observed startup, and retired snapshots were present:

```text
[agent/embedded] codex app-server log database rotated before startup
{
  "branch": "ghita/codex-log-retention",
  "command": "createIsolatedCodexAppServerClient",
  "realisticManagedCodexHome": "~/.openclaw/tmp/openclaw-codex-startup-proof-YWSrEz/agent/codex-home",
  "maxBytes": 4,
  "sawOriginalDbAtStartup": false,
  "sawRetiredDbAtStartup": true,
  "entries": [
    "logs_2.sqlite-wal.retired.2026-05-25T09-18-19-067Z",
    "logs_2.sqlite.retired.2026-05-25T09-18-19-067Z"
  ]
}
```

The committed startup-path test also passed:

```text
✓ |extensions| ../../extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts > shared Codex app-server client > rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts 32ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 15 skipped (16)
```

Supporting production incident proof: earlier in the same incident, pruning and VACUUM brought the affected OpenClaw-managed Codex DB from ~43 GiB / ~14.5M rows down to ~678 MiB with `remaining_old_rows=0`. A follow-up local retention run on the same affected install reported:

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "attention_required": false,
  "before_size_gib": 0.733,
  "after_size_gib": 0.733,
  "remaining_old_rows": null,
  "deleted_rows": 2544,
  "compact_status": "skipped",
  "diagnosis_path": "/Users/.../.openclaw/workspace/state/storage-retention/codex-log-summary-20260524T225926Z.json"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The branch startup path rotates an oversized realistic managed `CODEX_HOME` before the stdio app-server process observes startup state, and the affected local install no longer has old Codex log rows accumulating past the retention boundary after the adjacent local cleanup.
- **What was not tested:** I did not replace the production OpenClaw gateway binary with this branch on the Mac mini. This PR bounds OpenClaw-managed `CODEX_HOME` storage at app-server startup; native trace downsampling remains a separate follow-up.

## Config

- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_RETENTION=0|false|off|no` disables retention
- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=<positive integer>` overrides the default cap
- `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_RETIRED_LOG_SNAPSHOTS=<non-negative integer>` overrides retained retired snapshots

These are now documented in `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts --testNamePattern "rotates oversized CODEX_HOME logs before a stdio app-server process starts" --reporter verbose`
- `pnpm test:extension codex -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/log-retention.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts` — 64 files passed, 1,114 tests passed

## CI Note

The prior `checks-node-agentic-agents` failure was outside this PR surface. The failing log points at `src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts` and provider-runtime mock/export issues, not the Codex app-server retention files touched here. The new push will rerun normal CI.

## Follow-up

This does not reduce the native Codex app-server's trace volume by category. It prevents unbounded disk growth for OpenClaw-managed Codex homes. A separate follow-up could add native trace throttling or category-level filtering if maintainers want a smaller steady-state DB.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(agents): resolve CLI runtime in preflight + memory-flush gates
target.body:
## Summary

`runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` and `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` gate on `isCliProvider(params.followupRun.run.provider, params.cfg)`. `isCliProvider` only returns true when the passed provider id is itself a key in `agents.defaults.cliBackends`. For an agent configured with a primary model on a canonical provider (e.g. `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`) that resolves through a CLI runtime via `models.providers.<provider>.agentRuntime.id` (or per-agent / per-model `agentRuntime` policy), the gate evaluates `isCli=false` and preflight runs as if the agent were embedded — even though the conversation transcript and tokens live in the CLI runtime's own session, not in the OC transcript file.

This PR mirrors the resolution shape already used by the runtime dispatch path: resolve the CLI execution provider via `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider` first, then ask `isCliProvider` about the resolved id. The two preflight/flush gates predate that resolve and were never updated.

## Existing pattern

`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` resolves the CLI execution provider before gating on `isCliProvider`:

```ts
const cliExecutionProvider =
  resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider({
    provider,
    cfg: runtimeConfig,
    agentId: params.followupRun.run.agentId,
    modelId: model,
  }) ?? provider;

if (isCliProvider(cliExecutionProvider, runtimeConfig)) {
  // ... CLI dispatch path
}
```

The fix in this PR uses the same shape at both `agent-runner-memory.ts` call sites.

## Reproduction context

A long-lived agent routed through a CLI runtime via `agentRuntime.id` accumulates context inside the CLI's own session. The model reports a large `promptTokens` to OC, which OC stores as `entry.totalTokens`. Once that crosses the preflight threshold (`contextWindow - reserveTokensFloor - softThresholdTokens`), preflight fires and tries to compact the OC-side transcript file. For agents whose real conversation lives outside the OC transcript file (the CLI runtime's own session), OC's transcript file only contains delivery-mirror records, and the compaction safeguard's `containsRealConversationMessages` filter strips those, returning `compacted:false reason:"no real conversation messages"`. Preflight then throws `Preflight compaction required but failed: no real conversation messages`, surfacing as a generic dispatch error.

The runtime dispatch path doesn't hit this because it had already been migrated to `resolveCliRuntimeExecutionProvider`. The preflight + flush gates had not.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` green.
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`: 26 passed (existing 24 + 2 new). Both new tests fail without the source fix and pass with it.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` clean on both touched files.

The two new tests configure `agents.defaults.cliBackends["claude-cli"]` plus `models.providers.anthropic.agentRuntime.id = "claude-cli"` and assert that a `followupRun` with `provider="anthropic"` short-circuits both `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` (returns the entry without invoking `runEmbeddedPiAgent`) and `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` (returns the entry without invoking `compactEmbeddedPiSession`).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Telegram dispatch on a long-lived claude-cli-routed Anthropic agent surfaced "Something went wrong while processing your request." The underlying error in the gateway log was `Preflight compaction required but failed: no real conversation messages`. Root cause: `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` evaluated `isCliProvider("anthropic", cfg)` (false because cliBackends keys on `claude-cli`, not `anthropic`), so preflight ran on agents whose real conversation lives in claude-cli's session JSONL — not in OC's transcript file (which holds only delivery-mirror records for that surface). The compactor's `containsRealConversationMessages` filter then strips delivery-mirror records and returns `compacted:false`, so preflight throws.

**Real environment tested:** A live OpenClaw stack on macOS 14 with three claude-cli-routed Anthropic agents wired to Telegram. Anthropic configured as primary; routes through `auth.profiles["anthropic:claude-cli"]` so dispatch resolves to the claude-cli runtime. Six historical failures observed today across one of the three agents over ~2h before manual recovery (delete the broken session-store binding + archive the all-delivery-mirror transcript file).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Patched `~/.local/bin/openclaw-cli-runtime-preflight-gate-patch.py` applied to the brew-installed OC dist (mirrors this PR's source change exactly — same anchored swap of `isCliProvider(params.followupRun.run.provider, ...)` for resolve-then-isCli).
2. Restarted the gateway: `openclaw gateway restart --safe`.
3. Enabled debug logging via `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug` so the `preflightCompaction check` / `memoryFlush check` verbose lines emit.
4. Sent one Telegram inbound to a claude-cli-routed agent on the same session-key shape that previously failed.
5. Sent one Telegram inbound to a different claude-cli-routed agent for cross-agent confirmation.
6. Inspected the gateway log for the verbose `isCli=` value at both gates.
7. Reverted `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug` and restarted the gateway.

**Evidence after fix:**

Pre-fix gateway log (six historical failures across ~2h, redacted):

```
[compaction-diag] end runId=<redacted> sessionKey=<redacted-agent-session-key> diagId=cmp-<redacted>
  trigger=budget provider=anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 attempt=1 maxAttempts=1
  outcome=failed reason=unknown detail=No_API_key_found_for_provider_anthropic_._Auth_store:_<path>
  durationMs=7540
[compaction] skipping — no real conversation messages (sessionKey=<redacted>)
message dispatch completed: ... outcome=error duration=17203ms
  error="Error: Preflight compaction required but failed: no real conversation messages"
```

Pre-fix verbose `memoryFlush check` (debug log, before patch was applied; raw `provider="anthropic"` misclassified):

```
memoryFlush check: sessionKey=<redacted> tokenCount=51762 contextWindow=1048576 threshold=1024576
  isHeartbeat=false isCli=false
  persistedFresh=true ...
```

Post-fix verbose `memoryFlush check` (same env, same agent kind, debug log after patch + this PR's source change):

```
memoryFlush check: sessionKey=<redacted> tokenCount=48024 contextWindow=1048576 threshold=1024576
  isHeartbeat=false isCli=true
  ...
```

Post-fix preflight short-circuits at the gate before its own verbose log emits — the early-return at the resolved-runtime-isCli check fires before the `logVerbose` line on the run-preflight branch.

**Observed result after fix:** All three claude-cli-routed agents now resolve `isCli=true` at both `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` and `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded`. The Telegram dispatch path no longer hits the OC-transcript compaction path, no `Preflight compaction required but failed` errors, no user-visible "Something went wrong" message. Six previously-failing inbounds no longer reproduce on the affected session-key shape (verified by sending three test messages across two agents post-patch — all returned the agent's reply normally).

**What was not tested:** The auth-profile-pinned-to-direct-API-while-auth-order-prefers-CLI configuration was added in the follow-up commit (per Codex review) and is covered by the new regression tests but was not exercised live — the live test stack uses the simpler `auth.profiles["anthropic:claude-cli"]` mapping rather than a direct-API pin override. No Crabbox / Testbox run; the live verification was on a personal stack with the three claude-cli-routed agents reproducing the exact original failure shape.

target.title: fix(codex): compact group context without losing mission
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep compact group-channel Codex context focused on the current mission/request while preserving needed source-conversation metadata.
- Preserve session tool compatibility and document/configure the compact context behavior.
- Refresh prompt snapshots and focused runtime/config tests.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Group-channel Codex context remains bounded in live Discord sessions while the current request and source-conversation metadata remain available to the running agent.
- **Real environment tested:** Juan's local macOS OpenClaw install, stable package `OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)`, local gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, runtime `OpenAI Codex`, Discord group sessions under the `main` agent.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Exercised the compact-context runtime path through real Discord/Codex group sessions.
  2. Queried the live install with `openclaw --version`.
  3. Queried live gateway/session state with `openclaw status --json`.
  4. Checked the reported Discord Codex session token usage and gateway reachability.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  $ openclaw --version
  OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

  $ openclaw status --json

    "mode": "local",
  ```

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same run context.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head 8121fdb2cce09ebf383a267c8f0ea46c00aaca98, running on Linux with Node v26.0.0. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Created a temporary live-provenance-proof.tmp.ts harness in the PR worktree, registered a real trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and an exec/bash tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, then ran:

timeout 45s ./node_modules/.bin/tsx ./live-provenance-proof.tmp.ts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/openclaw-pr-91800-proof/live-provenance-proof.log

rg -n "[proof|[proof-policy|[proof-tool|[proof-result]|PASS|Error|externalContent" /tmp/openclaw-pr-91800-proof/live-provenance-proof.log

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] trusted policy ids=proof-before-tool-call-provenance-policy
[proof] registered real trusted tool policy in active plugin registry
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
No known gaps for the proof target. This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage; it specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path requested for this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

target.title: fix(agents): restore runtime context fallback for heartbeat prompts
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: During the `pi-embedded-runner` → `embedded-agent-runner` refactor, `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts` lost a legacy fallback. When `transcriptPrompt` is defined but not a substring of `effectivePrompt` (e.g. heartbeat placeholder `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]` vs exec event output), the effective text should be used as implicit `runtimeContext`. Without it, `buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage` skips creating the `custom_message`, and the model doesn't receive the runtime context on heartbeat turns.
- **Why now**: Discovered while investigating exec background completion notification behavior — exec events trigger a heartbeat wake, but the runtime context containing the exec result was silently dropped.
- **Outcome**: Restored the fallback so exec event data flows correctly through `runtimeContext` → `buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage` → `custom_message`.
- **Intentionally out of scope**: Persisting the custom_message across turns (current ephemeral design is intentional per 0503853c29 — only visible on the current turn).
- **Success**: Unit test covers the fallback path; temporary console.log in `attempt.ts` confirmed `runtimeContextForHook` is populated (303 chars) and `custom_message` is created.
- **Reviewer focus**: The condition `transcriptPrompt !== undefined && hiddenRuntimeContext === undefined && extracted.runtimeContext === undefined` — verify it correctly distinguishes "not found in text" from "found but no surrounding context."

## Linked context

No related issue directly — discovered while investigating exec background completion notification behavior.

This is a refactoring omission: the fallback was present in the old `pi-embedded-runner` and lost during the rename/restructure to `embedded-agent-runner`.

- Related: [#85341](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85341) — the refactor that moved `pi-embedded-runner` to `embedded-agent-runner`, where the fallback was dropped.
- Related: [#86875](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86875) — subsequent fix to `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts` for hook context handling.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Exec-event heartbeat wake does not inject runtime context `custom_message` into model messages.
- **Real environment tested**: Local gateway with `agent model: zte/Qwen3-235B-A22B`, ran exec command, triggered heartbeat.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Added temporary `console.log` at the `buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage` call site, rebuilt, ran gateway, executed `exec sleep 5 background=true`, waited for heartbeat wake.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture): 
  ```
  [runtime-context-v] hook present, chars: 303; 
  [runtime-context-v] custom msg created: true
  ```
  Full evidence (temp logging code + log output): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/91817#issuecomment-4666190162
- **Observed result after fix**: `runtimeContextForHook` contains the exec event text (303 chars), `buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage` successfully creates the `custom_message`.
- **What was not tested**: The actual model response to the custom_message (depends on provider/model behavior). Verification limited to confirming the custom_message object is built and injected into `messagesForCurrentPrompt`.
- **Proof limitations**: The custom_message is ephemeral (not persisted to transcript/jsonl), so proof relies on temporary logging at the construction site. The unit test in `runtime-context-prompt.test.ts` provides structural coverage of the extraction logic.
- **Before evidence**: Without the fix, `runtimeContextForHook` is `undefined` → `buildRuntimeContextCustomMessage(undefined)` returns `undefined` → no custom_message created. This is the current behavior on `main`.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts`: 22/22 pass.
- Added test `"uses effective text as implicit runtime context when transcriptPrompt is not found"` — covers the exact heartbeat/exec-event fallback scenario.
- All existing tests for marker-based runtime context extraction, modelPrompt splitting, and runtime-only events remain unchanged.
- Also ran live gateway test (described above) with temporary logging to verify the fix triggers on actual exec events.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** `No` — internal runtime context; only affects model-visible messages on exec-event heartbeat turns (restoring behavior that existed in pi-embedded-runner).
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`
- **What is the highest-risk area?** The fallback condition `transcriptPrompt !== undefined && hiddenRuntimeContext === undefined && extracted.runtimeContext === undefined` — if a future caller passes a `transcriptPrompt` that unintentionally doesn't match `effectivePrompt`, the entire effective text becomes `runtimeContext`. This is narrowly scoped and was the previous behavior.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** Unit test covering the exact fallback path; gated by three conditions that ensure it only fires when no other context mechanism provided data.

## Current review state

- **Next action**: Author ready for review.
- **Waiting on**: None.
- **Comments addressed**: N/A (first review).

target.title: fix(codex): restore memory recall from plugin tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Alternative to #91594; this draft leaves #91594 open and undisturbed.
- Restore native Codex memory recall by activating the selected memory plugin prompt capability only when the returned visible tools include the selected plugin's `memory_search` or `memory_get`.
- Keep the shared infrastructure touch narrow: visible selected memory tools and selected memory prompt capability must stay in sync with the same effective availability/runtime config used for tool visibility.
- Keep Codex prompt assembly passive: Codex builds the memory prompt text but does not force memory capability activation from the prompt path.
- No new config option, environment variable, CLI flag, public SDK/API surface, or Codex-local fallback recall policy.

## Linked context

Related: #91594, #87383

This is a narrower alternative to #91594. #87383 correctly stopped pasting raw native Codex workspace `MEMORY.md` into ordinary tool-enabled turns, but the Codex app-server path could still show memory tools without having the selected memory plugin prompt capability active. That made first-turn personalized recommendations skip saved constraints until the model corrected later.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Native Codex turns could receive searchable memory tools without using the selected memory plugin's recall guidance, so a first-turn personalized recommendation could ignore saved user constraints.
- Real environment tested: Exact current PR head `5f3800b075f56470ed248e05ae1f16f0bdb66433` on an OpenClaw gateway, via a Telegram direct route with `codex`, `memory-core`, and `telegram` loaded. Runtime path was the PR checkout build at `/home/<redacted>/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-tool-resolution/dist/index.js`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built runtime artifacts with `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`, restarted the OpenClaw gateway service from the PR checkout, sent a Telegram direct-route prompt asking which credit card to get next after meeting an Amex Platinum signup bonus, then inspected the Codex rollout and OpenClaw trajectory for that run.
- Evidence after fix (redacted runtime log and trajectory excerpt):

```text

runtime: OpenClaw gateway from PR checkout, Telegram direct route
loaded plugins included: brave, codex, memory-core, telegram

  2026-06-10T02:54:08Z http server listening (4 plugins: brave, codex, memory-core, telegram)
  2026-06-10T02:54:09Z gateway ready
  2026-06-10T02:54:10Z Telegram provider started

redacted inbound request:
  2026-06-10T02:54:59Z Telegram direct-route prompt asking which card to get next after the Amex Platinum signup bonus

compiled prompt checks:
  Deferred searchable OpenClaw dynamic tools listed=true
  memory_search mentioned=true
  memory_get mentioned=true
  ## Memory Recall present=true
  ## Codex Memory Tool Loading present=false
  raw root MEMORY.md paste present=false

Codex rollout:
  /home/<redacted>/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/codex-home/sessions/2026/06/10/rollout-2026-06-10T02-55-01-019eaf74-6603-74c2-898b-3be117a19d53.jsonl

OpenClaw trajectory:
  /home/<redacted>/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/01ad6fb1-25fe-4092-b75b-9b441c9800cc.trajectory.jsonl

first relevant tool events:
  2026-06-10T02:55:24.966Z tool.call memory_search query="credit cards Amex Platinum Chase Sapphire travel points card rewards 5/24 SUB bonus Ruben preferences" corpus="all" maxResults=5
  2026-06-10T02:55:25.373Z tool.result memory_search success=true
  2026-06-10T02:55:52.856Z tool.call memory_get path="memory/2026-05-20.md" from=3 lines=18
  2026-06-10T02:55:52.870Z tool.result memory_get success=true
  2026-06-10T02:55:57.925Z tool.call memory_get path="memory/2026-06-09.md" from=3 lines=8
  2026-06-10T02:55:57.936Z tool.result memory_get success=true
  2026-06-10T02:57:23.649Z tool.call message action="send"
  2026-06-10T02:57:25.626Z tool.result message success=true

redacted memory facts applied by the model before answering:
  existing card mix included an already-held Sapphire product
  no business-card applications
  Chase applications should be treated as off the board because of 5/24 status

visible answer behavior:

  explicitly accounted for the saved no-business-card, already-held-Sapphire, and 5/24 constraints before ranking options
```

- Observed result after fix: On exact PR head, Codex loaded the selected memory plugin recall guidance in the compiled prompt, called `memory_search` and `memory_get` before the first Telegram answer, read the relevant saved card-planning memory, and applied those saved constraints in the first recommendation.
- What was not tested: Other harness/channel paths were not live-tested. Not every memory engine was tested; the live proof used bundled `memory-core`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Private user, channel, and memory details are redacted. The run did not need the removed Codex-specific loading fallback: `## Codex Memory Tool Loading` was absent, while the selected memory plugin's `## Memory Recall` guidance was present and memory tools were still called before the answer.
- Before evidence: Before the fix, a Telegram direct-route run had `memory_search`, `memory_get`, and `tool_search` available, but `## Memory Recall` was absent; no memory tool calls were made before the answer, and the visible answer hedged on card eligibility rather than applying the saved history before recommending.

## Tests and validation

Current draft head validated: `5f3800b075f56470ed248e05ae1f16f0bdb66433`.

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-test-harness.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tools.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-test-harness.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/codex-native-task-runtime.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tools.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/memory-runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod && pnpm tsgo:test`
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`
- Exact-head live Telegram proof above after `node scripts/build-all.mjs qaRuntime`

Regression coverage added or updated:

- cached memory tool descriptors still activate the selected prompt capability
- same-named memory tools from a non-selected plugin do not activate the prompt capability
- effective runtime config is used for memory tool visibility and prompt activation
- disabled, denylisted, or manifest-filtered memory tools do not activate prompt capability
- stale prompt capability from a non-selected memory plugin is ignored
- Codex app-server memory recall guidance is present for root and daily memory paths without raw memory injection
- restored close-manager coverage remains no-bootstrap/no-reload

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes` for tool/prompt routing behavior only; no auth, secrets, network, or tool schema surface is added.

What is the highest-risk area? Native Codex memory tool visibility and selected memory prompt capability activation.

How is that risk mitigated? Activation is gated on already-visible selected-plugin `memory_search`/`memory_get` tools, uses the same effective availability/runtime config as tool visibility, ignores stale non-selected memory prompt capabilities, avoids new public SDK/config surface, and is covered by focused plugin and Codex app-server tests.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and PR CI on this draft.

Waiting on: ClawSweeper/maintainer review of the exact-head proof and the implementation concern around request-time registry activation.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper requested exact-head real Codex memory proof showing `memory_search` and `memory_get` before the first personalized answer. This body now includes exact-head Telegram direct-route proof for `5f3800b075f56470ed248e05ae1f16f0bdb66433`. ClawSweeper also raised a separate implementation concern around request-time registry activation; that code concern remains for maintainer review or a follow-up patch.

target.title: fix #69443: [Bug] Subagent RPC callback to WeChat session key routed to main session instead
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #69443.
- Treat a full agent-scoped `to` value, such as `agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq802hhmfc@im.wechat`, as the requested agent session key when no explicit `sessionKey` or `sessionId` is present.
- Apply the same session selector handling in the direct resolver, the agent CLI gateway dispatch path, and the generic gateway `agent` RPC handler, while clearing the delivery target after promotion so the session key is not reused as an outbound peer.
- Address the review finding for bare `/reset` / `/new` acknowledgements by sanitizing the promoted `to` in the reset ack path and rebuilding downstream delivery from the selected session's resolved channel target.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Subagent callback RPCs that pass a WeChat agent session key in `params.to` no longer default back to `agent:main:main`; the callback is accepted against the requested `agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq802hhmfc@im.wechat` session key. Bare reset acknowledgements with `deliver: true` also no longer reuse that full agent session key as an outbound peer target.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw repair worktree with installed dependencies, plus a real OpenClaw dev gateway process on `ws://127.0.0.1:19144` using an isolated OpenClaw home and config. This was not a mocked gateway call.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent/session.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; also ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "uses the selected session target for bare /reset delivery when to is an agent session key"`; then started the patched repair worktree dev gateway and ran `pnpm openclaw gateway call agent --url ws://127.0.0.1:19144 --json --timeout 10000 --params '{"message":"daily-fix current-head proof for session-key routing","to":"agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq802hhmfc@im.wechat","idempotencyKey":"proof-90231-current-head-to-session-key","timeout":1}'` with the local proof gateway token configured for that isolated process.
- **Evidence after fix:** Targeted Vitest passed 2 shards: gateway shard 292 tests passed and commands shard 72 tests passed. The focused reset ack regression passed in both gateway Vitest projects. The live gateway call returned `{"runId":"proof-90231-current-head-to-session-key","sessionKey":"agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq802hhmfc@im.wechat","status":"accepted"}`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The gateway accepted the agent RPC and reported the same WeChat agent-scoped session key that was supplied in `params.to`, proving it did not resolve the callback to `agent:main:main`. The reset ack regression proves the promoted session selector is sanitized before delivery and the ack is sent to the selected session's stored WeChat target instead of to the full `agent:` key string.
- **What was not tested:** I did not perform an external WeChat network round trip or use a third-party WeChat account. The verified boundary is the OpenClaw gateway `agent` RPC callback path, reset ack delivery planning, and session selection behavior that caused this issue.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** `status: ⏳ waiting on author` is addressed by this update: the PR now includes the requested author-side repair, fresh verification, and an updated body mapping each review item.
- **RF-002:** Addressed. Added focused gateway handler regression coverage for bare `/reset` with `deliver: true` and `to` equal to a full agent session key.
- **RF-003:** Addressed. Bare `/reset` / `/new` acknowledgement delivery no longer uses the full `agent:` session key as an outbound peer; the reset ack path now sanitizes the promoted selector and routes delivery from the selected session target.
- **RF-004:** Addressed with a focused automated repair only; no product decision, config change, protocol change, or broader channel behavior change was needed.
- **RF-005:** Addressed in `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`. The reset ack branch passes a sanitized request when `to` was promoted, and the helper rebuilds downstream delivery from the selected session's resolved channel, target, account, thread, and delivery mode.
- **RF-006:** Addressed. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent/session.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; result: 2 Vitest shards passed, with gateway shard 292 tests passed and commands shard 72 tests passed.
- **RF-007:** Addressed. Ran `git diff --check HEAD~2..HEAD`; result: passed with no output.
- Added regression coverage for the direct resolver, CLI gateway dispatch normalization, and generic gateway `agent` RPC handler so the same session-key selector behavior is covered at each entry point.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/commands/agent/session.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression scenario is `to` / `--to` containing `agent:main:openclaw-weixin:direct:o9cq802hhmfc@im.wechat` with no explicit `sessionKey` or `sessionId`; the expected behavior is selecting that session key and not treating it as a delivery target. The reset ack extension locks the bare `/reset` + `deliver: true` path so acknowledgement delivery uses the selected session's stored channel target.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The failure happens before external WeChat transport, at the resolver, CLI dispatch, gateway handler ingress, and reset ack delivery planning boundaries. The tests cover those boundaries directly without adding a broad channel fixture or unrelated session migration setup.
- Added a resolver test proving an agent-scoped `--to` value is returned as the requested session key.
- Added a CLI gateway dispatch test proving an agent-scoped `--to` value is forwarded as `params.sessionKey` and removed from `params.to`.
- Added a gateway handler test proving `params.to` with an agent-scoped session key loads that session and does not pass the same key as a delivery target.
- Added a gateway reset ack test proving bare `/reset` delivery uses the selected session target when `to` was an agent session key.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Without this fix, callback replies from subagents to channel-specific sessions can silently continue the wrong main session, losing the intended WeChat conversation context. Without the reset ack follow-up, `/reset` or `/new` callbacks could still attempt to deliver an acknowledgement to the full `agent:` session key string instead of the WeChat peer.
- **What did NOT change:** No config surface, schema, protocol field, session-store format, provider default, WeChat transport adapter, or plain external `to` delivery behavior changed. Out of scope: external WeChat account delivery, session migrations, and unrelated channel routing behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical source-of-truth for distinguishing full agent session keys remains `classifySessionKeyShape()`, and explicit `sessionKey` / `sessionId` still outrank `to`. This patch moves that canonical classification ahead of context normalization in each ingress path, then keeps reset ack delivery aligned with the already-selected session route rather than adding a downstream string fallback.
- **Related open PR scan:** No broader same-contract or canonical PR was identified for this issue before the task branch was prepared; this PR stays limited to the gateway/agent session selector contract for complete `agent:` keys and the same contract's reset ack delivery branch.
- **Risk labels considered:** Gateway/session routing and message-delivery behavior; no public protocol field, config surface, schema, migration, or provider default changes.
- **Risk explanation:** The changed interpretation only applies when `to` is already a complete `agent:` session key and neither `sessionKey` nor `sessionId` is explicitly set. Plain destination values still use the existing delivery/session derivation behavior. The reset ack change only affects the already-promoted session-selector case and reuses the selected session's resolved delivery target.
- **Why acceptable:** The promoted value is already in OpenClaw's canonical agent session-key shape, so preserving it as the selector matches the caller's intent and avoids the direct-sender normalization path that caused the bug. Reset acknowledgements should follow the selected session route, not the raw selector string.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the reported gateway callback routing bug and the reviewed reset ack branch; the remaining proof gap is only the external WeChat transport round trip, not the local gateway session selector or reset ack delivery behavior.
- **Patch quality notes:** This is a narrow root-cause change, not a retry, timeout increase, fallback, or downstream guard. It fixes the session selector decision before session loading and delivery planning, then keeps reset ack delivery on the selected session route.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source invariant was broken at ingress: a complete agent-scoped session key carried in `to` was treated as a direct destination/context value instead of as a session selector. Because non-group direct contexts are normalized to the agent main session, the callback could load `agent:main:main` instead of the WeChat session key. The reset ack branch had a second path that still read the original request's `to` after promotion.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix restores the session-selection contract at the source by classifying `to` before context-based normalization and promoting full `agent:` session keys to `sessionKey` only when no stronger selector is present. The gateway handler clears the delivery `to` after promotion, and the reset ack helper now rebuilds its downstream delivery request from the selected session's resolved channel target, preserving the invariant that the selected session and outbound delivery target cannot diverge for this callback path.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same run context.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head 8121fdb2cce09ebf383a267c8f0ea46c00aaca98, running on Linux with Node v26.0.0. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Created a temporary live-provenance-proof.tmp.ts harness in the PR worktree, registered a real trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and an exec/bash tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, then ran:

timeout 45s ./node_modules/.bin/tsx ./live-provenance-proof.tmp.ts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/openclaw-pr-91800-proof/live-provenance-proof.log

rg -n "[proof|[proof-policy|[proof-tool|[proof-result]|PASS|Error|externalContent" /tmp/openclaw-pr-91800-proof/live-provenance-proof.log

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] trusted policy ids=proof-before-tool-call-provenance-policy
[proof] registered real trusted tool policy in active plugin registry
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
No known gaps for the proof target. This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage; it specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path requested for this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

## Summary
The `image generate` CLI command was missing the `--file` option that `image edit` already supported. This PR adds `--file` support to achieve parity, forwarding input files to the image generation runtime.

Fixes #91734.

## Changes
- `src/cli/capability-cli.ts`:
  - Added `"--file"` to `image.generate` capability metadata flags
  - Added `.option("--file <path>", "Input file", collectOption, [])` to the `image generate` command registration
  - Wired `opts.file` through to `runImageGenerate({ file: files })`

- `src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`:
  - Added regression test: `forwards input files for image generate`
  - Added regression test: `reports the expanded image.generate flags in capability inspect`

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts --run` — 97 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/cli/capability-cli.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts` — clean
- `pnpm build` — clean
- `pnpm format:fix` — clean

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw infer image generate --file <path>` now accepts and forwards input files to the generation runtime, matching the existing `image edit --file` behavior.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node.js 22, local source checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `pnpm test src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts --run`
- **Evidence after fix**: All 97 tests pass, including the two new regression tests that verify `--file` is accepted by the CLI parser and forwarded to `generateImage` as `inputImages`.
- **Observed result after fix**: `image generate --file <path> --prompt "..."` correctly resolves the file and passes it to the runtime.
- **What was not tested**: Live provider image generation with actual API keys; only CLI parsing and runtime forwarding is tested.

target.title: fix(logging): prune non-idle stale diagnostic session states
target.body:
## Summary

When `requestStuckSessionRecovery()` fails with `status: "failed"`, the diagnostic session state stays non-idle forever because `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState()` returns `false` and `applyRecoveryOutcomeToDiagnosticState()` returns early. The entry accumulates as a ghost in `diagnosticSessionStates` indefinitely.

This PR transitions `status: "failed"` recovery outcomes to idle — **only when the diagnostic state generation is still current** (no newer recovery has modified the entry). The existing stale-idle prune logic then removes them naturally. Other non-mutating outcomes (`skipped`, `already_in_flight`) and stale-generation failed outcomes are preserved.

Fixes #91697

## Changes

- **`src/logging/diagnostic-session-recovery-coordinator.ts`**: In `applyRecoveryOutcomeToDiagnosticState()`, when `outcome.status === "failed"` AND `isDiagnosticSessionStateCurrent()` confirms the generation has not changed, transition the entry to `"idle"` with `queueDepth = 0`. 1 file, +11 lines.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Failed stuck-session recovery leaves diagnostic entries permanently non-idle. After fix: failed + current generation → idle transition; failed + stale generation → preserved; skipped → preserved.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, openclaw commit 3b658bb297. tsx v4.22.3.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Wrote proof script importing `diagnosticSessionStates`, `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates`, `getDiagnosticSessionState`, `peekDiagnosticSessionState`, `isDiagnosticSessionStateCurrent` and `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState`
2. Script tests 3 cases: failed+current generation, failed+stale generation, skipped noop
3. Ran `npx tsx scripts/proof-91721-v5.mjs`

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture):**

```
$ npx tsx scripts/proof-91721-v5.mjs

============================================================
recovery-path ghost state — generation guard proof
============================================================
now  = 2026-06-10T12:00:00.000Z
TTL  = 30 minutes

--- Simulating recovery outcomes ---

Case 1: failed + generation current
  gen: request=0 current=0
  after: state=idle queueDepth=0

Case 2: failed + generation STALE
  gen: request=2 (stale) current=0 (newer recovery)
  after: state=processing (generation guard → PRESERVED)

Case 3: skipped noop
  after: state=processing (unchanged)

--- After prune ---
entries remaining: 2 (failed-stale-gen + skipped)

--- Verification ---
failed+current → idle                ✓
failed+current → pruned              ✓
failed+stale-gen preserved           ✓
failed+stale-gen still present       ✓
skipped state preserved              ✓

✓ All checks passed.
exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** 3 entries → failed+current transitions to idle and is pruned. Failed+stale-gen (generation mismatch) is preserved — a newer recovery owns this entry. Skipped noop is preserved unchanged. The generation guard ensures only entries whose state has not been modified since the request was created are transitioned to idle.

**What was not tested:** Full gateway restart with stuck-session recovery, heartbeat-driven stuck-session detection.

target.title: fix(memory): do not prune session index from a failed directory scan
target.body:
## Summary

A full memory sync treats source enumerations as the source of truth for **destructive** maintenance: stale-row pruning of the session and memory-file indexes (`files` + `chunks` + vectors + FTS), full reindex rebuilds, and exported-session reconciliation. These enumerations swallowed filesystem errors and returned an empty listing, so a single transient scan failure (NFS `EIO`/`ESTALE`, permission blip) was indistinguishable from an empty directory and wiped the corresponding index — followed by an expensive full re-read/re-embed after the next successful scan. The failed-scan prune test in this PR fails on current `main` (the indexed row gets deleted), confirming the hazard is live.

### Sessions (original scope)

- `packages/memory-host-sdk`: add `scanSessionFilesForAgent(agentId): { ok, files }`. `ok: true` means the directory was actually read or authoritatively does not exist (see below); `ok: false` means the scan failed. `listSessionFilesForAgent` delegates to it and is unchanged for its non-destructive callers.
- `resolveMemorySessionSyncPlan` takes a required `scanOk` and returns `pruneStaleRows`, true only for a full enumeration that authoritatively read the directory.
- `syncSessionFiles` gates the stale-row prune on `pruneStaleRows`. A failed scan skips the prune (with a warning) and leaves the index intact for the next successful enumeration.

### Follow-up hardening (from review)

- A missing sessions dir is authoritative-empty only when the parent agent dir exists: fresh agents (the dir appears on first transcript write) prune orphans without log noise, while an unreachable state tree (e.g. unmounted volume) counts as a failed scan.
- `MemorySourceSyncPlan` carries `scanOk`; `runSync` no longer clears `dirty`/`sessionsDirty`/`sessionsDirtyFiles` after a failed scan, so the next eligible sync retries instead of reporting a clean sync that indexed nothing.
- The memory-file sibling gets the same guard: new `scanMemoryFiles(): { ok, files }` (exported via `engine-storage` and the plugin SDK barrel) with explicit root `readdir` probes — fs-safe `walkDirectory` swallows readdir errors even at the walk root, so an lstat-only check would still report `ok` on the exact failure class this PR targets. `syncMemoryFiles` skips its stale-row prune on a failed enumeration.
- Full reindexes abort (`assertReindexScanOk`) instead of swapping in an empty index built from a failed scan; the safe path restores the original DB on throw, the unsafe path skips `writeMeta` so the next sync retries the rebuild.
- `exportSessions` skips its reconcile-delete pass on a failed listing so one blip cannot delete the exported session-markdown corpus.
- Renamed the local CLI diagnostics in `cli.runtime.ts` (`scanMemoryFiles` → `diagnoseMemoryFiles`, `scanSessionFiles` → `diagnoseSessionFiles`) to avoid colliding with the new SDK export surfaced through the same barrel.
- Fixed a stale assertion in `manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts` that fails on `main` today: it still expects the pre-#89138 `void` return of `syncSessionFiles`.

### Why skip on *failed*, not on *empty*

Deletions/resets archive to usage-counted `.deleted.`/`.reset.` artifacts that still appear in the listing, and `disk-budget` can physically remove the last archive — so an **authoritatively empty** directory with a non-empty index is legitimately reachable. In that case we must still prune, or deleted-session content would linger in search forever. Skipping only on a *failed* scan prevents the wipe without introducing persistent orphan rows.

### Known residual gaps (follow-ups)

- Subtree readdir failures below the probed roots remain invisible (`@openclaw/fs-safe` `walkDirectory` reports no errors); blast radius is the affected subtree, not the whole index. A clean fix needs an additive fs-safe API (e.g. `WalkDirectoryResult.failedDirCount`).
- `resolveCanonicalRootMemoryFile` swallows workspace-root readdir errors; bounded to the single root memory file.

## Verification

Worktree rebased on `origin/main` (`48ec58a584`).

target.title: fix(ui): reload WebChat history for deferred thinking
target.body:
Fixes #91727.

## Summary

WebChat now reloads the selected transcript at the end of a run when a deferred persisted `session.message` already contains assistant thinking/reasoning but the live final chat payload only contains renderable answer text. That restores the post-run repaint that makes persisted reasoning visible without a manual browser refresh, while preserving the no-reload path for normal source-reply finals.

## Root cause

The final chat-event path avoided `loadChatHistory()` whenever a terminal final event carried renderable assistant text, even if an earlier `session.message` during the same active run had the persisted assistant message with thinking content. That meant the live UI kept the final text payload and never re-projected the transcript thinking block until the user manually refreshed.

## What changed

- Track whether a deferred, selected assistant `session.message` contains thinking content while a chat run is active.
- Treat a final event as needing history reload when that deferred transcript thinking is missing from the final live payload.
- Keep the scope tight to the selected/equivalent session and selected agent/global alias so stale or cross-session finals do not reload the visible chat.
- Ignore user messages that contain literal thinking-like text.
- Preserve the existing source-reply no-flicker path when there is no deferred transcript thinking to recover.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: a persisted assistant message containing a `thinking` block arrives via `session.message` during an active WebChat run, then the terminal `chat` final event carries only renderable assistant text. Before this fix, the final payload stayed live and the thinking block remained hidden until a manual refresh. After this fix, the final event calls `loadChatHistory()` once and the selected chat state is replaced with the persisted transcript containing the thinking block.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `260f6a5aec349889942965d24a76fe687efd3818` on macOS using the repo Node/pnpm toolchain, plus the actual Control UI running in headless Google Chrome through the repo Vite e2e server. The browser connected to a gateway-shaped WebSocket endpoint, received the same event order from the report (persisted assistant `session.message` with thinking during an active run, then a terminal `chat` final payload), requested `chat.history`, and repainted WebChat from the persisted transcript without a manual refresh.

target.title: fix(memory): do not prune session index from a failed directory scan
target.body:
## Summary

A full memory sync treats source enumerations as the source of truth for **destructive** maintenance: stale-row pruning of the session and memory-file indexes (`files` + `chunks` + vectors + FTS), full reindex rebuilds, and exported-session reconciliation. These enumerations swallowed filesystem errors and returned an empty listing, so a single transient scan failure (NFS `EIO`/`ESTALE`, permission blip) was indistinguishable from an empty directory and wiped the corresponding index — followed by an expensive full re-read/re-embed after the next successful scan. The failed-scan prune test in this PR fails on current `main` (the indexed row gets deleted), confirming the hazard is live.

### Sessions (original scope)

- `packages/memory-host-sdk`: add `scanSessionFilesForAgent(agentId): { ok, files }`. `ok: true` means the directory was actually read or authoritatively does not exist (see below); `ok: false` means the scan failed. `listSessionFilesForAgent` delegates to it and is unchanged for its non-destructive callers.
- `resolveMemorySessionSyncPlan` takes a required `scanOk` and returns `pruneStaleRows`, true only for a full enumeration that authoritatively read the directory.
- `syncSessionFiles` gates the stale-row prune on `pruneStaleRows`. A failed scan skips the prune (with a warning) and leaves the index intact for the next successful enumeration.

### Follow-up hardening (from review)

- A missing sessions dir is authoritative-empty only when the parent agent dir exists: fresh agents (the dir appears on first transcript write) prune orphans without log noise, while an unreachable state tree (e.g. unmounted volume) counts as a failed scan.
- `MemorySourceSyncPlan` carries `scanOk`; `runSync` no longer clears `dirty`/`sessionsDirty`/`sessionsDirtyFiles` after a failed scan, so the next eligible sync retries instead of reporting a clean sync that indexed nothing.
- The memory-file sibling gets the same guard: new `scanMemoryFiles(): { ok, files }` (exported via `engine-storage` and the plugin SDK barrel) with explicit root `readdir` probes — fs-safe `walkDirectory` swallows readdir errors even at the walk root, so an lstat-only check would still report `ok` on the exact failure class this PR targets. `syncMemoryFiles` skips its stale-row prune on a failed enumeration.
- Full reindexes abort (`assertReindexScanOk`) instead of swapping in an empty index built from a failed scan; the safe path restores the original DB on throw, the unsafe path skips `writeMeta` so the next sync retries the rebuild.
- `exportSessions` skips its reconcile-delete pass on a failed listing so one blip cannot delete the exported session-markdown corpus.
- Renamed the local CLI diagnostics in `cli.runtime.ts` (`scanMemoryFiles` → `diagnoseMemoryFiles`, `scanSessionFiles` → `diagnoseSessionFiles`) to avoid colliding with the new SDK export surfaced through the same barrel.
- Fixed a stale assertion in `manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts` that fails on `main` today: it still expects the pre-#89138 `void` return of `syncSessionFiles`.

### Why skip on *failed*, not on *empty*

Deletions/resets archive to usage-counted `.deleted.`/`.reset.` artifacts that still appear in the listing, and `disk-budget` can physically remove the last archive — so an **authoritatively empty** directory with a non-empty index is legitimately reachable. In that case we must still prune, or deleted-session content would linger in search forever. Skipping only on a *failed* scan prevents the wipe without introducing persistent orphan rows.

### Known residual gaps (follow-ups)

- Subtree readdir failures below the probed roots remain invisible (`@openclaw/fs-safe` `walkDirectory` reports no errors); blast radius is the affected subtree, not the whole index. A clean fix needs an additive fs-safe API (e.g. `WalkDirectoryResult.failedDirCount`).
- `resolveCanonicalRootMemoryFile` swallows workspace-root readdir errors; bounded to the single root memory file.

## Verification

Worktree rebased on `origin/main` (`48ec58a584`).

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src` — **860/860 passing**, including the new `manager-sync-ops.prune-guard.test.ts` (both sources: failed scan ⇒ no rows pruned and `scanOk: false`; authoritatively-empty / absent-dir ⇒ orphan rows pruned) and `manager-session-sync-state.test.ts` (`pruneStaleRows` matrix).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` on touched host-sdk files (`session-files.test.ts`, `internal.test.ts`) — passing (scan ok / authoritatively-empty / absent-dir-with-parent / unreachable-tree / failed readdir-after-lstat / failed extra path; `listSessionFilesForAgent` and `listMemoryFiles` unchanged on failure). Full `packages/memory-host-sdk` run has one unrelated under-load timeout flake in untouched `retry-utils.test.ts`; it passes in isolation.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:test:packages`, `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` — clean.
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts`, plugin-sdk boundary lints (`no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`), `sync-plugin-sdk-exports --check` — clean.
- `oxfmt --check` + oxlint on all changed files — clean.
- Hazard proof on `main`: running this PR's failed-scan prune test against clean `origin/main` deletes the indexed session row (`expected ['sessions/main/thread.jsonl'] to deeply equal []`).

### Known gaps (CI/Testbox)

- Full `pnpm build` not run locally in the worktree — recommend CI coverage.
- `plugin-sdk:surface:check` / `plugin-sdk:api:check` not re-run locally after the final rebase (both were failing on pre-rebase `main` against stale baselines; `main` has since regenerated them). If CI flags the two intentional new exports (`scanSessionFilesForAgent`, `scanMemoryFiles`), a `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` baseline bump can ride along.

## Notes

- #91081 (session-listing cache perf PR, still open) is complementary: it reduces READDIR load; this PR makes the destructive consumers stop trusting failed listings. No conflicts beyond both touching `session-files.ts` (additive here).

target.title: fix(logging): release stuck session lanes after drained abort
target.body:
Fixes #91700.

## Summary

Stuck-session recovery now resets the session lane when an active embedded/reply run aborts and drains cleanly but the diagnostic session still reports queued work. This covers the `aborted=true`, `drained=true`, command-lane `queuedCount=0`, diagnostic `queueDepth>0` wedge where the old recovery cycle returned `released=0` and left the agent silent until later watchdog cycles.

## Root cause

`recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` only reset the session lane after abort/drain when the command-lane snapshot had queued work, no active session existed, or abort/drain failed. The queued inbound work reported by diagnostics lives in session state (`queueDepth`) and can be nonzero even when command-lane `queuedCount` is zero, so a cleanly aborted dead run skipped lane reconciliation.

## What changed

- Treat nonzero diagnostic session `queueDepth` as a reason to reset the resolved session lane after recovery has already cleared or aborted the active work.
- Preserve the existing active-owner safeguard: active embedded/reply work is still observe-only unless active-abort recovery is enabled or stale-progress reclaim applies.
- Preserve the unregistered active-lane safeguard: if there is no registered active session but the command lane still has active work, recovery keeps the lane instead of resetting it.
- Update focused recovery coverage for both active embedded handles and active reply work without a handle.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` with `queueDepth=1`, a clean `aborted=true` / `drained=true` result, and command-lane `queuedCount=0` now releases the lane on the first recovery cycle instead of returning `released=0`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at `upstream/main` head `9a1f2022b127f27312eeb7f57e42d477bddf2c19` plus this patch, on macOS, using the repo Node/pnpm toolchain. The deterministic proof drives the real `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` module in the logging runtime test harness so the diagnostic queue-depth and session-lane reset contract can be isolated.
